The court may affirm the action of such authority or may set the same aside if it finds that such authority acted illegally or abused its discretion.

(d) Each municipality shall become a member of the International Code Council and shall pay the membership fee.

documents.heading: Sec. 29-298a. Fire Marshal Training Council established. Duties. Members.

chapter.name: Building, Fire and Demolition Codes. Fire Marshals and Fire Hazards. Safety of Public and Other Structures

documents.text: There shall be established within the Department of Administrative Services a Fire Marshal Training Council which shall advise the State Fire Marshal and the Codes and Standards Committee on all matters pertaining to (1) certification training programs, (2) decertification hearings, (3) in-service training for fire marshals in the state, and (4) programs for all other persons eligible to receive training pursuant to subsections (a) to (c), inclusive, of section 29-251c. The council shall be composed of nine members as follows: The State Fire Marshal or the State Fire Marshal's designee; a member of the Codes and Standards Committee to be elected by such committee; three members designated by the Connecticut Fire Marshals Association, one of whom shall be a part-time paid local fire marshal, deputy fire marshal or fire inspector, and two of whom shall be full-time local fire marshals, deputy fire marshals or fire inspectors; one member designated by the Connecticut Chapter of the American Institute of Architects; one member appointed by the Office of Education and Data Management within the Department of Administrative Services; one member designated by the Connecticut Conference of Municipalities; and one member designated by the Connecticut Council of Small Towns. The council may elect such officers as it deems necessary.

documents.heading: Sec. 29-423. Fire safety standard and firefighter protection act enforcement account.

chapter.name: Building, Fire and Demolition Codes. Fire Marshals and Fire Hazards. Safety of Public and Other Structures

documents.text: There is established a separate, nonlapsing account to be known as the “fire safety standard and firefighter protection act enforcement account”. The account shall contain all certification fees submitted by holders in accordance with section 29-419, any civil penalties imposed in accordance with subsection (d) of section 29-418 or subsection (d) of section 29-420, and any other moneys required by law to be deposited in the account. The proceeds of the account shall be used by the State Fire Marshal solely to fund the processing, testing and administrative activities specified in sections 29-418, 29-419 and 29-420.

documents.heading: Sec. 29-453b. Bleeding control trainer qualification program. Bleeding control training.
documents.text:
(a) The Department of Emergency Services and Public Protection may administer a bleeding control trainer qualification program in each district department of health formed pursuant to section 19a-241. If the department administers such trainer qualification program, such trainer qualification program shall (1) utilize a training model developed by a nonprofit organization that specializes in bleeding control, as amended from time to time, and (2) provide qualification in bleeding control training to allow participants who successfully complete such program to provide bleeding control training to other individuals.

(b) If the department administers the trainer qualification program pursuant to subsection (a) of this section, the director of health for each district department of health shall determine the eligibility criteria for participation in such program, and participants shall be members of the following groups within such district: (1) Employees of such health department, (2) employees of youth service bureaus, (3) school employees, as defined in section 10-221o, (4) employees and volunteers of youth-serving organizations, (5) employees and volunteers of operators of youth athletic activities, as defined in section 21a-432, (6) employees of municipal social service agencies, (7) members of paid municipal or volunteer fire departments, (8) members of local police departments, (9) any other municipal employees, and (10) members of local and regional boards of education. The trainer qualification program may be included as part of an in-service training program for school employees provided pursuant to section 10-220a.

(c) Any individual who is currently qualified as a bleeding control trainer through the trainer qualification program administered pursuant to subsection (a) of this section may provide bleeding control training to any member of the public.

(d) The Department of Emergency Services and Public Protection may partner with a nonprofit organization that specializes in bleeding control to carry out the provisions of this section.

(e) No fee shall be charged to any individual or member of the public for participating in (1) any trainer qualification program administered pursuant to subsection (a) of this section, or (2) a bleeding control training provided pursuant to subsection (c) of this section.
